preview_text:
The model favors South Korea with a 61% win probability, leaving South Africa at 13% and a draw at 26%. South Korea holds a commanding edge of 48 percentage points over its opponent, placing the underdog in a difficult position.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a heavily skewed matchup. South Korea's probability sits well clear of the field, while the draw at 26% remains plausible enough to suggest the match will not inevitably run away as a one-sided affair. Still, South Africa faces steep odds of emerging victorious; draws occur frequently enough in group-stage football that the outcome space is not entirely binary.

Model and market are closely aligned across all three outcomes. The model's South Korea estimate sits 2.4 percentage points above the sportsbook consensus, while the draw probabilities match almost exactly. Polymarket sentiment tracks similarly to the consensus odds. The dashboard presents these as consistent signals, though the marginal gaps remain within typical variance between estimation methods.
